PHASE 2 - AGENT EXECUTION LAYER
Mental Model (Important)
Phase 1 was:
"Every tool call is a durable event."
Phase 2 adds:
"An agent is a graph of tool calls with intent, constraints, and memory."
Agents do not execute tools directly.
They execute plans, and plans compile down into MCP executions.

New Crates Added
crates/
+-- fusion-agent-spec/
+-- fusion-agent-graph/
+-- fusion-agent-runtime/
+-- fusion-agent-tests/

fusion-agent-spec - Stable Agent Contract
This is the ABI of agents. Lock it early.
fusion-agent-spec/Cargo.toml
[package]
name = "fusion-agent-spec"
version = "1.0.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
fusion-agent-spec/src/lib.rs
use serde::{Serialize, Deserialize};

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone)]
pub struct AgentStep {
    pub id: String,
    pub tool: String,
    pub input: serde_json::Value,
    pub rationale: String,
    pub cost_estimate: u64,
}

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone)]
pub struct AgentPlan {
    pub plan_id: String,
    pub steps: Vec<AgentStep>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum AgentState {
    Pending,
    Running,
    Paused,
    Completed,
    Failed(String),
}
?? Why this matters:
This allows explainability without LLM hallucinations.
The rationale is stored, replayed, audited.

fusion-agent-graph - Execution Graph + Resume
crates/fusion-agent-graph/Cargo.toml
[package]
name = "fusion-agent-graph"
version = "1.0.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
fusion-agent-spec = { path = "../fusion-agent-spec" }
crates/fusion-agent-graph/src/lib.rs
use fusion_agent_spec::{AgentPlan, AgentState};

pub struct AgentGraph {
    plan: AgentPlan,
    cursor: usize,
    state: AgentState,
}

impl AgentGraph {
    pub fn new(plan: AgentPlan) -> Self {
        Self {
            plan,
            cursor: 0,
            state: AgentState::Pending,
        }
    }

    pub fn next_step(&self) -> Option<&fusion_agent_spec::AgentStep> {
        self.plan.steps.get(self.cursor)
    }

    pub fn advance(&mut self) {
        self.cursor += 1;
        if self.cursor >= self.plan.steps.len() {
            self.state = AgentState::Completed;
        } else {
            self.state = AgentState::Running;
        }
    }

    pub fn pause(&mut self) {
        self.state = AgentState::Paused;
    }

    pub fn state(&self) -> &AgentState {
        &self.state
    }

    pub fn explain_step(&self) -> Option<String> {
        self.next_step().map(|s| s.rationale.clone())
    }
}
?? Partial execution is just a cursor.
No magic. No async spaghetti.

fusion-agent-runtime - Budgeted, Policy-Aware Execution
This crate binds agents to the Phase 1 runtime.
crates/fusion-agent-runtime/Cargo.toml
[package]
name = "fusion-agent-runtime"
version = "1.0.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
fusion-agent-graph = { path = "../fusion-agent-graph" }
fusion-agent-spec = { path = "../fusion-agent-spec" }
fusion-runtime = { path = "../fusion-runtime" }
fusion-mcp-spec = { path = "../fusion-mcp-spec" }
crates/fusion-agent-runtime/src/lib.rs
use fusion_agent_graph::AgentGraph;
use fusion_mcp_spec::McpRequest;
use fusion_runtime::Runtime;
use fusion_policy::Policy;

pub struct AgentRuntime<P: Policy> {
    runtime: Runtime<P>,
    budget_remaining: u64,
}

impl<P: Policy> AgentRuntime<P> {
    pub fn new(runtime: Runtime<P>, budget: u64) -> Self {
        Self { runtime, budget_remaining: budget }
    }

    pub fn execute_plan(&mut self, graph: &mut AgentGraph) -> Result<(), String> {
        while let Some(step) = graph.next_step() {
            if step.cost_estimate > self.budget_remaining {
                graph.pause();
                return Ok(());
            }

            let req = McpRequest {
                id: step.id.clone(),
                tool: step.tool.clone(),
                input: step.input.clone(),
            };

            self.runtime.execute(req)?;
            self.budget_remaining -= step.cost_estimate;
            graph.advance();
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
?? Cost budgeting is enforced at runtime, not advisory.

Multi-Agent Coordination (Shared Tool Graphs)
This is intentionally simple - and powerful.
use std::collections::HashMap;

pub struct AgentCoordinator {
    shared_tools: HashMap<String, usize>, // tool ? usage count
}

impl AgentCoordinator {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self { shared_tools: HashMap::new() }
    }

    pub fn register_use(&mut self, tool: &str) {
        *self.shared_tools.entry(tool.to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;
    }

    pub fn tool_pressure(&self, tool: &str) -> usize {
        *self.shared_tools.get(tool).unwrap_or(&0)
    }
}
This becomes the foundation for:
• Trust-weighted tool selection
• Load-aware agents
• Cooperative planning

Agent Tests (Determinism Verified)
crates/fusion-agent-tests/src/lib.rs
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use fusion_agent_spec::*;
    use fusion_agent_graph::*;

    #[test]
    fn agent_plan_advances_deterministically() {
        let plan = AgentPlan {
            plan_id: "test".into(),
            steps: vec![
                AgentStep {
                    id: "1".into(),
                    tool: "tool_a".into(),
                    input: serde_json::json!({}),
                    rationale: "Step A".into(),
                    cost_estimate: 1,
                }
            ],
        };

        let mut graph = AgentGraph::new(plan);
        assert!(graph.next_step().is_some());
        graph.advance();
        assert!(matches!(graph.state(), AgentState::Completed));
    }
}

What Phase 2 Achieves
Fusion now supports:
Explainable agent plans
Partial execution & resume
Budget enforcement
Deterministic replay compatibility
Multi-agent coordination primitives
Policy-gated agent execution
At this point:
Fusion is not "running tools".
Fusion is executing intentions under constraint.
That is the difference between a chatbot backend and an agent operating system.
